meta.title: CAPA Register — Permit-to-Work Isolation Nonconformity, Maintenance Shop, Plant 3
meta.subtitle: Corrective & preventive action lifecycle vs ISO 45001 10.2 — ingested from a safety-audit finding set; de-identified; competent-person review required
meta.author: HSE Department
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers in the ingested register and supporting evidence (names, signatures, training/injury records) were pseudonymised before any analysis; the re-identification key is held separately, access-controlled, and is not part of this document. No injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published. This CAPA register is decision-support; a competent person must validate it before closure.

sections.text: This is the management (B7) side of the CAPA lifecycle for nonconformity NC-1, ingested from the safety-audit finding set (the B6 register: action, owner, due, measure, links_to_cause, hoc_tier). The audit found that the permit-to-work procedure does not mandate an independent isolation-verification step before sign-off (4 of 12 sampled permits signed off with no recorded isolation check) — a systemic gap traced to cause RC-1 (an absent procedural control), not an isolated lapse. For each cause this register carries BOTH a corrective action (fix this occurrence) AND a preventive action (stop recurrence), every action hierarchy-of-controls ranked, owned, ISO-dated, measured, traced to its cause, and given a scheduled effectiveness verification. No CAPA is marked verified-effective without a recorded check. The whole lifecycle runs within one invocation (stateless-per-run; v1.0). This register is de-identified throughout; the re-identification key is held separately.

metrics.label: Highest-order preventive control
metrics.caption: above an admin-only fix (hierarchy of controls)

items: Source (Q0): an audit finding set INGESTED from safety-audit (the B6→B7 seam). The finding id (NC-1), the cause id(s) (RC-n), and the first CAPA rows were lifted verbatim — the producer's {action, owner, due, measure, links_to_cause, hoc_tier} schema is reused, not re-keyed.

Lifecycle: ingest → structure → validate → schedule → emit, within a single invocation. No persistent cross-session CAPA store is maintained (that is a v2 consideration); the status and verification fields are designed so a future store can read them without a schema change.

Validation: smart_actions.validate_register over the producer's six fields (owner + ISO due + measure + links_to_cause on every action); controls.rank_controls / validate_treatment over the full corrective + preventive set (the ppe_admin_only flag forces a higher-order control or a recorded justification).
De-identification re-check: the ingested register was re-scanned, not assumed clean; all personnel carry role labels only.

Schema rule: the lifecycle fields capa_type / verification / status are B7-side ADDITIVE metadata that smart_actions.validate_register ignores — they are NEVER moved into smart_actions.REQUIRED_FIELDS (that would fork the A7 engine and break the round-trip).

rows.title: RC-1 (root)
rows.description: Organisational / procedural: the PTW procedure omits an independent isolation-verification step before sign-off, so permits were signed off with no recorded isolation check — a systemic control gap, not an individual error. Reused from the ingested audit cause (not re-investigated).

rows.title: RC-2 (contributing)
rows.description: The permit close-out lacked a mandatory sign-back field, so 2 of 12 permits were closed without a final sign-back.

sections.text: Managed CAPA register (B5 schema + B7 lifecycle fields)

Each row carries the producer's six fields verbatim (action, owner, due, measure, links_to_cause, hoc_tier — validated by smart_actions.validate_register) EXTENDED additively with the three B7 lifecycle fields the engine ignores: capa_type (corrective | preventive), verification (the scheduled effectiveness check — method · due · status), and status (open · in-progress · verified-effective · verified-ineffective · closed). The same smart_actions.validate_register that the producer ran accepts this register unchanged — the round-trip holds with zero A7 change.

rows: Stand down sign-off on any permit lacking a recorded isolation check until isolation is independently confirmed (interim control while the procedure is amended).
0 permits signed off without a recorded isolation check during the interim period (100% sample)
Fit an isolation-verification interlock so a permit cannot be signed off until isolation is independently confirmed in the register.
0 sampled permits signed off without a recorded isolation check at the next quarterly audit (no recurrence over the audit window)
Re-sign the 2 permits closed without a final sign-back and confirm the work was correctly closed out (fix this occurrence).
Both affected permits re-signed and verified closed (2/2)
Add a mandatory close-out sign-back field that blocks permit closure if blank.
0 permits closed without a sign-back in the following month's sample (no recurrence)
Stand down sign-off without a recorded isolation check (interim)
Re-sign the 2 permits closed without sign-back
Mandatory close-out sign-back field that blocks closure if blank

rows.control: Isolation-verification interlock — the permit cannot be signed off until isolation is independently confirmed (RC-1 preventive)
Mandatory close-out sign-back field that blocks permit closure if blank (RC-2 preventive)
Amend the PTW procedure to mandate an independent isolation-verification step before sign-off; interim stand-down until done (RC-1 corrective)
Re-sign and verify closure of the affected permits (RC-2 corrective)

sections.text: Effectiveness verification & schedule (the lifecycle B7 owns)

Every CAPA carries a verification floor of method + due + status. No CAPA may be marked verified-effective without a recorded check (method + due + status + verified_by + verified_date). smart_actions.days_until_due(verification.due, today) surfaces any verification past its due date in the register view (a negative value = overdue). A re-occurrence window (e.g. 'no recurrence at the next quarterly audit') is recorded where practicable as the stronger effectiveness test — encouraged, not hard-required in v1.0.

rows: Not yet — cannot close verified-effective until the check is recorded
Not yet — no-recurrence window runs to the next quarterly audit

sections.text: Closure rule (ISO 45001 10.2): a CAPA is not closed verified-effective until its scheduled effectiveness check is recorded (method + due + status + verified_by + verified_date). All four CAPAs above remain open / in-progress pending their scheduled verification — none may be reported as effective yet.

items: ISO 45001 clause 10.2 (incident, nonconformity & corrective action) — the CAPA-lifecycle backbone: react → evaluate the need to eliminate the cause so it does not recur → implement corrective + preventive action → review effectiveness → retain documented evidence.
ISO 45001 clause 8.1.2 (hierarchy of controls) — applied to every corrective and preventive action; the preventive controls favour Engineering over an administrative-only fix.

Jurisdiction: the documented-information / corrective-action / record-retention duty is cited from the matched KB row when a regulatory regime applies (India → the resolved state duty via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS, mandatory state detection, never a national form number). This management-system CAPA cites the ISO clauses + the org procedure.
Re-identification key held separately; no injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published in this circulated register.
